items.title: Former St Mary's Hostel site recognised as key Stolen Generations place

children.content: Former residents say the heritage listing of the St Mary's Hostel site in Alice Springs is an important step towards healing, truth-telling and remembering the children taken there under assimilation-era policies.

image.altText: A woman in a light grey jumper wearing sunglasses stands in front of a large tree

items.title: Alice Springs drug and alcohol rehab curbed due to flooding

children.content: Drug and Alcohol Services Australia is running programs at a vastly reduced capacity due to flooding at its Alice Springs site earlier this year.

image.altText: The camera looks across the street at the DASA site behind a green fence, with cars out the front on the curb.

items.title: Injuries and fires reported after NT cracker night celebrations

children.content: People across the Northern Territory have spent the night setting off their own fireworks for the Territory's annual cracker night celebrations, lighting up the sky with colour and keeping firefighters busy putting out blazes.

gallery.altText: green and pink fireworks light up the sky over water with a crowd on the shore watching
red fireworks going off over the ocean
Three children wave orange flags with fireworks going off in the background

thumbnail.altText: green and pink fireworks light up the sky over water with a crowd on the shore watching
red fireworks going off over the ocean
Three children wave orange flags with fireworks going off in the background

items.title: Pair behind dramatic flood rescue awarded for going 'beyond the call of duty'

children.content: A helicopter pilot and a police officer who performed a daring flood rescue in Katherine earlier this year have been awarded for their actions as part of the Northern Territory's Territory Day awards.

image.altText: Man in high-vis sits on top of a 4WD vehicle half submerged in floodwater, holding a dog, as a helicopter hovers next to them.

items.title: Cattle sale in Alice Springs nets $7m as market surges

children.content: The strong results at the Alice Springs sale reflect the overall Australian cattle market, which has surged in recent weeks.

image.altText: a close up image of a black steer

items.title: NT power, water and sewerage prices increase by 5.3 per cent from today

children.content: The Northern Territory treasurer has defended an increase in power, water and sewerage prices, which are set to rise by 5.3 per cent from today.

image.altText: Power lines on a leafy green street.

items.title: Today in history: NT town of Katherine gazetted 100 years ago

children.content: Today marks 100 years of the NT town of Katherine, which has a rich history as a meeting place and crossroads to the north and as home to some of Australia's most stunning natural sites.

image.altText: A group of Russian men and women are sitting on a tractor in a historical photo

items.title: Teenagers say vapes are still commonplace and easily accessible

children.content: The ABC has spoken to a number of teenagers who say despite a crack down on vapes, they remain accessible and commonplace.

gallery.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

thumbnail.altText: Disposable vapes sitting on a countertop.

items.title: 'Recurring issues' uncovered following surveillance of chopper sector

children.content: Australia's aviation watchdog has warned that cutting corners on maintenance and certification requirements could create a "real safety risk".

image.altText: A chopper shot from below, it is parked on green grass and a blue sky can be seen in the background.

items.title: 'Hyperscale' data centre planned for remote NT

children.content: The proposed 19,150-hectare 'Project Ares' would be built by Energy North on Murranji Station in the Barkly Region, as up to 12 proponents eye the NT for data centres.

image.altText: a gate with a cut-out of a man on a horse silhouetted against a sunset.

items.title: Study finds NT residents are the nation's happiest

children.content: Northern Territory residents are the happiest in the nation, a new university study has found, with access to nature, warm weather and cultural diversity among the theories for Territorians' sunny attitudes.

gallery.altText: A woman, smiling, standing in a CBD mall.

thumbnail.altText: A woman, smiling, standing in a CBD mall.

items.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help find earthquake survivors in Venezuela

children.content: A week after devastating twin earthquakes struck Venezuela rescue teams are using drones, dogs, cameras and specialised listening equipment to search for survivors.

articles.title: Satellite images show Venezuelan earthquakes wiped out entire towns

children.content: Venezuela's devastating earthquakes flattened buildings and coated parts of the northern coast in dust and debris, satellite imagery shows.

image.altText: An aerial image of rows of residential homes and an ocean-side boat marina with significant earthquake damage.

gallery.altText: A man walks among the rubble of a building that collapsed in earthquakes.
A man in a yellow search helmet crawls out from a mound of rubble.
A man in a yellow search helmet crawls out from a mound of rubble.

thumbnail.altText: A man walks among the rubble of a building that collapsed in earthquakes.
A man in a yellow search helmet crawls out from a mound of rubble.
A man in a yellow search helmet crawls out from a mound of rubble.

items.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart

items.title: Privacy Commissioner ordered to hand over report on Amex security failures

children.content: The Privacy Commissioner has been ordered by parliament to hand over a secret report detailing its investigation into American Express's technology security failures, after using gag orders and legal threats to keep it confidential.

image.altText: Close-up photo of the square blue American Express logo on the corner of a yellow credit card.

items.title: Pictures show interior of Trump's new Air Force One jet

children.content: The jet, gifted to the US government by Qatar, has been retrofitted, with Trump shaking off concerns over the cost.

gallery.altText: Trump speaks to press ahead of boarding his new presidential plane to North Dakota.
Brunch plate posted by Karoline Leavitt shows the first meal served on Trump's new plane.
Pictures taken from the new presidential plane by inaugural passengers.

thumbnail.altText: Trump speaks to press ahead of boarding his new presidential plane to North Dakota.

items.title: Professor admits wanting university job to benefit his consulting firm

children.content: ICAC hears that John Dewar wanted his UOW appointment to benefit KordaMentha and his acknowledgement there could be a perception of a conflict of interest.

image.altText: Four panel screen shot, man in focus in large shot sitting in a chair

items.title: NDIS fraud 'gaps remain open' as integrity report handed down

children.content: A Labor-led parliamentary inquiry into fraud in the NDIS has made 12 recommendations to improve the integrity of the scheme.

image.altText: Jenny McAllister in the Senate

items.title: Venezuelans make desperate pleas for help a week after devastating double earthquake

children.content: Venezuelans in the coastal city of La Guaira are pleading for heavy machinery to assist in ongoing rescue operations more than a week after a double earthquake devastated the area.
Magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 quakes devastated the area one week ago and at least 2,200 people have died.
People on the ground have faced a desperate wait for help to find family members lost under the rubble.
Frustration is growing in the community as they ask where the government is, and why help has not arrived.

items.title: Lions hold on for win over Geelong in Kardinia classic

children.content: Brisbane Lions earn a second straight win over Geelong at Kardinia Park in a high-scoring epic, as the Lions jump into the top four.

image.altText: Two Brisbane Lions AFL players grin and look at each other after a goal, as Geelong players look dejected.

items.title: Vatican excommunicates members of right-wing breakaway group SSPX

children.content: The Vatican has excommunicated priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval.

image.altText: Pope Leo in gold and white robes holding up a golden goblet, over a stone platform with other silver goblets.

items.title: Nobody knows where John Frum came from but he is god in this village

children.content: On Vanuatu's Tanna Island, I immerse myself in a culture that worships a shape-shifting spirit known as John Frum.

gallery.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

thumbnail.altText: Kate dances with a woman in a field in Vanuatu.

items.title: Jackass began with a gunshot. Now it's a $500m franchise

children.content: For more than 25 years, prank franchise Jackass has been delighting teens and terrifying parents. Now the gang is heading to the cinemas for what could be their last ride.

image.altText: A bull gores a man dressed as a matador

items.title: Matthew Flinders’s steamy letters reveal a hidden side to the man who named Australia

children.content: Flinders circumnavigated and named Australia. Impressive stuff, but he also wrote many impassioned letters and lived a life full of crushes.

image.altText: A letter from 1800, cursive ink writing and faded paper

items.title: Quick hits: Feuding Belgians save the day, England claims 'disadvantage'

children.content: Belgium stars turn on each other before combining to save their team, England makes claims of "disadvantage", and Congo's goalkeeper gives it all he's got. Here are today's World Cup quick hits.

articles.title: USA's World Cup dream alive despite red card against Bosnia and Herzegovina

children.content: The US seals progression to the last 16 with a 2-0 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina despite losing a man to a red card, while Belgium converts a penalty in extra-time to beat Senegal 3-2.

image.altText: Folarin Balogun points to his forehead in celebration

articles.title: When do the Socceroos play next and how to watch the knockout match

children.content: Australia's journey at the 2026 World Cup continues this weekend as the Socceroos take on Egypt. Here's everything you need to know.

image.altText: Players line up and sing their national anthem.

articles.title: Mile Jedinak knows what it's like to take a World Cup penalty

children.content: Now an assistant coach for the Socceroos, and with a penalty shootout a possibility when Australia face Egypt in the round of 32, Mile Jedinak knows what it takes to seize the moment.

image.altText: Jedinak scores penalty against the Netherlands

gallery.altText: Leandro Trossard and Youri Tielemans, Lionel Mpasi and Jude Bellingham

thumbnail.altText: Leandro Trossard and Youri Tielemans, Lionel Mpasi and Jude Bellingham

items.title: Coalition MP calls out gambling advertising carve-out

children.content: Liberal MP Simon Kennedy argues the federal government's proposed changes to gambling advertising laws contain a streaming loophole.

image.altText: Man in light blue shirt sits in from of radio mic and looks into distance. He is wearing headphones.

items.title: Eight Thai monks killed after 11yo crashes parent's truck into procession

children.content: An 11-year-old has driven his parents' truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing eight monks.

image.altText: A wrecked car near an ambulance on the side of a road.

items.title: MP's company fined $555k over worker's quad bike death

children.content: The court heard the 82-year-old worker was not wearing a helmet and the quad had no rollover protection when the crash occurred.

image.altText: A woman in a blue shirt holding a hat standing in front of some sheep.

items.title: 'At greatest risk of CTE': Expert calls out dangers of heading the ball

children.content: With all eyes on the World Cup, Australian football's management of the risks associated with the practice of heading the ball, such as dementia and CTE, is in the spotlight.

image.altText: A young soccer player about to kick a soccer ball.

items.title: Ex-Sydney childcare worker jailed for 'disturbing and depraved' child abuse

children.content: A painstaking victim identification process was undertaken by Australian Federal Police to establish his offending occurred at six of out-of-school hours care centres over a three-year period.

image.altText: A CCTV still of David James buying a drink at the Orchard Hotel.

items.title: Australia ranks third-highest for median wealth globally, UBS data shows

children.content: Australia holds the third-highest median wealth globally at $306,074, pipped at the post by Luxembourg and Belgium.

image.altText: Office workers cross the street in Sydney

items.title: Teen accused of abducting 17yo with intent to force marriage

children.content: A teen has been accused of abducting a 17-year-old girl from near a Melbourne school with the intention of forcing her to marry him.

image.altText: A blue sleeve with a Victoria Police badge sewn into it, including the motto "Uphold the Right".

items.title: Russia strikes in central Kyiv kill at least 18 people and injure dozens

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

image.altText: A building is damaged and on fire.

items.title: Fans hope to see Thunderbirds win netball grand final for third time

children.content: Among the devoted Thunderbirds fans making the journey to Melbourne for the grand final against the Vixens are the Rawlins family, including sisters Tess and Eve.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a smiling woman with a dark background

items.title: Major milk company fined for misleading consumers about fresh milk

children.content: One of Australia's largest milk providers has been fined by the consumer watchdog for misleading consumers with "fresh milk" labels on products that contained substantial amounts of powdered milk.

image.altText: Milk pouring into a cup with soft green background

items.title: Why F1's rumour mill is speculating futures of Piastri and Verstappen

children.content: F1's rumour mill has been in overdrive in recent times about Oscar Piastri's future at McLaren, with his boss having to hose down suggestions the Australian will be driving anything other than a Papaya-coloured F1 car in 2027.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri and Max Verstappen sitting next to each other during the drivers' photo
